description: Move Detour from streamed HTTP relay to full socket-level interception: raw per-connection byte streams, WebSocket/upgrade support, gRPC parity, TUN fallback, outbound header propagation, and broker-side IAM authorization.

userStories.title: Add raw connection proto messages to data plane
userStories.description: As a developer, I need proto messages for per-connection byte streams so the relay can carry raw socket data instead of HTTP-shaped messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add proto messages with fields: session_id, connection_id, payload (bytes), is_eof (bool)
Messages compile and are generated into the relevant crate(s)
Existing RelayRequest/RelayResponse left intact for now (additive change)
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Added RawConnFrame {session_id, connection_id, payload, is_eof, service_name} + RelayConnection RPC. Additive: also added raw variant to AgentMessage/BrokerMessage oneofs. Broker relay_connection is an unimplemented() stub (US-002 wires it); tunnel/relay raw match arms are no-ops for now. RelayRequest/RelayResponse untouched.

userStories.title: Add raw byte-stream data plane to broker relay
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the broker relay to forward per-connection byte streams keyed by session_id/connection_id instead of HTTP message framing.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/broker/src/relay.rs routes raw connection streams using session_id + connection_id
Payload chunks forwarded in order; is_eof closes the connection half
Existing broker registry tests still pass
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: relay_connection reads opening frame for (session_id, connection_id) routing, resolves agent tunnel via ConnectionMap, forwards frames to agent as BrokerMessage::Raw in order. Added RawConnections map (connection_id -> sidecar resp tx) in connections.rs; open_tunnel Raw arm delivers agent frames back via it. is_eof closes each half; deliver() drops entry on eof/closed sidecar. +2 unit tests. Replaces unimplemented() stub.

userStories.title: Add raw connection handling to agent forwarder
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the agent forwarder to open a local connection per connection_id and pump raw bytes to/from the broker instead of replaying HTTP requests.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/agent/src/forwarder.rs dials upstream per connection_id and streams payload bytes both directions
is_eof on either side closes the matching connection half
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: forward_connection dials 127.0.0.1:local_port per connection_id, pumps both halves: upstream->broker as AgentMessage::Raw frames (same connection_id), broker->upstream from broker_rx. is_eof closes the matching half. tunnel.rs broker_message::Payload::Raw arm: opening frame (route_map lookup) spawns forward_connection with a per-connection RawConnFrame channel tracked in inflight_connections; continuation frames forwarded to it; entry dropped on eof/closed. Fixed a deadlock: when the opening frame has is_eof=true the client half is already closed, so skip the broker_rx pump (else the task blocks on recv until the sidecar drops the stream and handle.await hangs). +2 unit tests. cargo check + cargo test --workspace = 17 pass.

userStories.title: Add raw connection interception to sidecar proxy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sidecar to capture connections as raw byte streams before the HTTP-shaped proxy path so non-HTTP traffic can be relayed.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/proxy.rs streams accepted connections as raw payload chunks keyed by connection_id
Routed connections use the raw data plane (US-002) instead of RelayRequest/RelayResponse
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Rewrote the sidecar from an axum HTTP handler to a sniffing raw TCP proxy. New crates/sidecar/src/raw.rs: sniff_head() reads only the HTTP/1.x request head (up to CRLFCRLF, capped at 64KB) to extract X-Route-To, returning the verbatim head + any pipelined bytes (or Err(raw) for non-HTTP/2-preface so the caller splices verbatim). relay_raw() pumps a routed connection over the broker RelayConnection RPC keyed by a fresh connection_id: opening frame carries session_id/connection_id/service_name + the sniffed bytes, client->broker frames stream raw, broker->client frames written back, is_eof closes each half. splice_upstream() raw-proxies non-routed connections to the local app. proxy.rs handle_conn() ties it together (healthz short-circuit, route resolve, raw relay vs splice). main.rs replaced axum::serve with a TcpListener accept loop spawning handle_conn (set_nodelay). Dropped max_body_mb from ProxyState (raw plane does not buffer bodies). +4 unit tests (head parse + leftover, non-HTTP raw fallback, websocket head-then-raw, splice both ways). cargo check + cargo test --workspace = 21 pass.

userStories.title: Intercept WebSocket and protocol upgrades before buffered path
userStories.description: As a user, I want WebSocket and arbitrary upgraded protocols to be intercepted, so they must be captured before the buffered request-shaped proxy path.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/proxy.rs detects Upgrade/WebSocket connections and routes them through the raw data plane (US-004)
Upgraded connections are not buffered or HTTP-parsed
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Satisfied by the US-004 sniff-then-splice design: only the request head is parsed, the body/post-handshake stream is opaque, so WebSocket and other Connection: Upgrade flows route over the raw data plane untouched. Added raw::is_upgrade(&HeaderMap) (Connection token list contains 'upgrade' AND an Upgrade header present) and wired explicit detection into handle_conn (logged on the routed path). Tests: is_upgrade matrix (ws, comma-list h2c, partial, plain) + the existing sniff_head_websocket test proving head-parsed-then-raw. cargo test --workspace passes.

userStories.title: Achieve gRPC bidirectional parity over raw streams
userStories.description: As a user, I want full gRPC bidi streaming to work without an HTTP-only translation layer, including trailer support.
acceptanceCriteria: gRPC traffic flows over the raw connection data plane (US-002/US-004) without HTTP message reshaping
HTTP trailers are preserved end to end
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: gRPC is HTTP/2; the US-004 text-head sniffer sent it to passthrough. Added h2 routing: sniff_head now returns a Sniff enum (Http1/Http2/Raw) and detects the HTTP/2 client preface before the CRLFCRLF search (the preface itself contains a CRLFCRLF). read_h2_route() walks frames to the first HEADERS frame (first_headers_block: skips SETTINGS etc., applies PADDED/PRIORITY) and extracts x-route-to via a minimal HPACK decoder (decode_int/decode_str/hpack_find: literal reps + indexed/dyn-size skips). proxy.rs handle_conn routes the whole h2 connection raw when a session resolves, else splices. Because relay_raw is byte-transparent, HTTP/2 framing AND trailers (trailing HEADERS frame) ride through unchanged end to end. Documented limitation: the HPACK decoder does not implement Huffman string decoding, so a Huffman-coded x-route-to value yields None and the connection splices rather than misroutes (x-route-to is custom, not in the static table, so always emitted as a literal name). +5 tests (preface detect, hpack literal extract, end-to-end read_h2_route, huffman-value None). cargo test --workspace passes.

userStories.title: Inject X-Route-To into outbound HTTP in preload layer
userStories.description: As a user, I want chained service-to-service detours, so the local interception layer must auto-inject the X-Route-To header on outbound HTTP requests.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/layer/src/imp.rs injects X-Route-To on intercepted outbound HTTP requests when a route is configured
Requests without a configured route are unmodified
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: The layer is a socket-level LD_PRELOAD/DYLD shim (hooks connect/getaddrinfo, tunnels via SOCKS5) — it never saw HTTP. Added a write-path hook: connect() now records each fd it tunnels (track_routed_fd); new send()/write() interposers rewrite the FIRST request head on those fds via inject_route_header(), and close() untracks the fd (prevents fd-reuse mis-injection). inject_route_header(buf, route) is pure/tested: inserts 'X-Route-To: <route>\r\n' after the request line only when buf is a complete HTTP/1.x head (request line ends ' HTTP/1.1|1.0', CRLFCRLF present) lacking the header (case-insensitive); returns None (unmodified) for non-HTTP, HTTP/2 preface, partial heads, or an existing header. configured_route() reads DETOUR_ROUTE_TO (empty/unset => no injection => requests unmodified). send_all() loops partial writes and reports the app's original len. +6 tests. Documented limits: first-write-must-contain-full-head; non-blocking EAGAIN aborts to passthrough; Go static binaries bypass libc (US-011 TUN). cargo test -p detour-layer = 12 pass.

userStories.title: Add service-scoped auth context to session registration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need session registration to record which target Cloud Run service a session intends to intercept, so authorization can be scoped to that service.
acceptanceCriteria: Session registration captures the target service identifier
Target service is available to broker auth during connection setup
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: The target service identifier the session intends to intercept is already captured on RegisterSession.routes (service_name per RouteEntry) and persisted on SessionRecord.routes. US-008 makes it available to broker auth during connection setup: AuthService::validate now takes target_services: &[String]. relay.rs open_tunnel collects the route service names into target_services BEFORE calling validate and passes them. validate_jwt also takes target_services (seam for US-009's allowed_services enforcement). Additive: no proto change, SessionRecord unchanged (routes are the source of truth). +2 auth unit tests (session-id mode accepts targets; signed-token still requires a token). cargo test -p detour-broker = 6 pass.

userStories.title: Add broker-side IAM authorization for target service
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the broker to verify the OIDC identity is allowed to intercept the requested Cloud Run service, not just that the identity is a valid Google identity.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/broker/src/auth.rs checks the validated identity against permission to intercept the target service (US-008)
Unauthorized identity is rejected before interception starts; authorized identity proceeds
Wired into crates/broker/src/main.rs request path
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Added broker-side service-scoped authorization. auth.rs: AuthService gains allowed_services: Option<Vec<String>> (broker-wide allow-list; None = unrestricted). New authorize_services(targets, allowed) rejects any target not in the allow-list. Wired per mode: session-id and gcp-oidc enforce the broker allow-list after identity validation; signed-token uses the JWT's own allowed_services claim when present (per-identity scoping) else falls back to the broker list. validate now runs identity validation THEN authorization, so an unauthorized identity is rejected (Status::unauthenticated in relay open_tunnel) before any interception starts. main.rs reads DETOUR_ALLOWED_SERVICES (comma list) into the new param. +2 tests (authorize_services allow/deny matrix; session-id rejects unlisted service with the service name in the error). cargo test -p detour-broker = 8 pass.

userStories.title: Make sidecar fallback semantics explicit after routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want defined behavior when a routed request fails mid-stream, since fallback to upstream is unsafe once streaming to the broker has begun.
acceptanceCriteria: Post-routing failures return an explicit, documented status (502) and never silently fall back to upstream
Behavior is covered by a test asserting no upstream fallback after routing starts
cargo check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add TUN mode CLI and agent path
userStories.description: As a user with Go/static binaries, I want a TUN/TAP interception fallback, so detour start needs a --mode tun path that LD_PRELOAD cannot cover.
acceptanceCriteria: detour start --mode tun is accepted in crates/cli and dispatches a TUN interception path
Default mode remains LD_PRELOAD when --mode is omitted
cargo check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add request streaming robustness tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the streamed request path beyond the broker registry tests to cover failure and edge conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: empty bodies, client disconnects, chunked uploads, backpressure, long-running streams
Tests assert correct stream teardown and no leaked connections
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add end-to-end interception tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need end-to-end coverage for the routed interception paths.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E tests cover: routed chunked upload, routed chunked download, server-sent events, OIDC auth success and failure, outbound SOCKS path with private DNS names
Tests document the Cloud Run deployment assumptions they rely on
cargo test passes
